refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR focuses on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is already solution-neutral, specifying what the system must achieve without prescribing how. There are no multiple obligations connected by 'and' or 'or' that require splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation.
The NGHE solution shall ensure communication quality meets the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service (QoS) parameters to meet packet loss requirements
Incorporate real-time network monitoring and dynamic adjustment of transmission parameters to ensure packet loss stays below threshold
Use buffering and smoothing techniques at the receiver to compensate for packet loss and maintain teleoperation quality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on network conditions and error correction algorithms
Interaction between retransmission logic and teleoperation control loop
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates the communication quality management within the protocol layer, supporting independence from other system functions. Adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms can be designed modularly to meet the packet loss requirement without tightly coupling to other system components.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters and do not degrade unexpectedly
Risk that adaptive mechanisms may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness
Assumes error correction and retransmission can be tuned to meet the ≤1% packet loss target without violating safety policies
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including latency and jitter constraints in the FR to ensure teleoperation quality beyond just packet loss, enabling more comprehensive DP selection.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core communication and networking functionalities, including protocol implementation and adaptive error correction mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional network interface for sending and receiving packets, essential for implementing communication protocols with error correction and retransmission.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs status and performance data related to communication quality and error correction effectiveness.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the communication protocol and error correction logically belong here; minimal diffusion since retransmission and error handling are core to this module.
Vehicle module uses communication but does not implement protocol logic; would cause high diffusion if protocol implemented here.
Teleoperation depends on communication but is more about control commands; protocol implementation here would diffuse across other modules.
Safety Services may monitor communication but should not implement protocol; poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission, as it centralizes networking functions and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Network Link interface stability and performance.
If error correction parameters need frequent tuning by other modules, some diffusion may occur.
Integration with Telemetry for monitoring may require careful interface design to avoid coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within the customer-defined limit, fulfilling the functional requirement of supporting teleoperation with required communication quality.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Network Link interface stability and performance.
If error correction parameters need frequent tuning by other modules, some diffusion may occur.
Integration with Telemetry for monitoring may require careful interface design to avoid coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is highly cohesive and appropriate for containing the communication protocol and adaptive error correction mechanisms, minimizing diffusion risks. Other modules have lower containment scores and higher diffusion risks, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the ownership boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface and ensuring compliance with the ≤1% packet loss acceptance criterion under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM boundary is clear and logical, as this module encompasses the core communication functionalities required to meet the FR.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Telemetry to ensure monitoring does not introduce coupling.
Establish protocols for tuning error correction parameters to minimize diffusion across modules.
Monitor and manage dependencies on the Network Link interface to maintain stability and performance.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is adequately addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The NGHE Solution module is a suitable ownership boundary with manageable diffusion risks, and verification feasibility is confirmed. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to avoid unnecessary decomposition that could complicate verification and increase diffusion risks. Recommended revisions focus on interface contracts and dependency management rather than further decomposition.

revise.why: To explicitly define interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Telemetry, establish protocols for tuning error correction parameters, and monitor dependencies on the Network Link interface to minimize diffusion and maintain stability.

assumptions: The current FR fully captures the required communication quality for teleoperation.
The NGHE Solution module can encapsulate all necessary functionalities without further subdivision.
Monitoring and tuning mechanisms can be effectively managed via interface contracts.
open_risks: Dependency on Network Link interface stability and performance.
Potential diffusion if error correction parameters require frequent tuning by other modules.
Coupling risks with Telemetry monitoring interfaces.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing packet loss metrics consistently meet ≤1% under agreed network conditions.
Documentation of interface contracts and tuning protocols.
Performance and stability reports of Network Link interface integration.
